title: Ears Get the Royal Treatment Today!
story: Your ears deserve a celebration, and March 13 is their special day! National Earmuff Day honors those fuzzy, warm ear-huggers that keep us cozy in winter. People celebrate by wearing their favorite earmuffs, sharing photos of colorful or silly designs, and appreciating this simple but brilliant invention. Some folks even wear earmuffs indoors just for fun on this day!
activity_question: What do people do to celebrate National Earmuff Day?
activity_choices: Wear their favorite earmuffs and share photos
Listen to loud music all day
Cover their ears and ignore everyone
activity_answer: Wear their favorite earmuffs and share photos
bonus_fact: Earmuffs were invented by a 15-year-old boy in 1873!

title: Heroes in Everyday Clothes
story: People across the country celebrate National Good Samaritan Day on March 13 by doing kind acts for strangers who need help. Some folks carry groceries for elderly neighbors, help someone change a flat tire, or pay for a stranger's coffee. Schools organize kindness challenges where students look for ways to lend a hand. The holiday encourages everyone to be like the helpful person from an old story who stopped to help someone in trouble when others just walked by.
activity_question: What might someone do to celebrate National Good Samaritan Day?
activity_choices: Eat only green foods all day
follow_up_question: What's the kindest thing a stranger has ever done for you?

title: Sparkle and Shine on Jewel Day!
story: Every March 13, people celebrate National Jewel Day by showing off their favorite sparkly treasures! Some wear their fanciest jewelry to work or school, while others visit museums to admire famous gems and diamonds. Jewelry stores often have special sales, and many people take time to clean their rings, necklaces, and bracelets until they gleam. It's the perfect day to appreciate both precious stones and costume jewelry that make us feel special!
activity_question: What do people often do to their jewelry on National Jewel Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite piece of jewelry and why does it make you feel special?

title: Breaking Bad Luck Rules with Umbrellas!
story: Pop open that umbrella right in your living room because March 13 is National Open an Umbrella Indoors Day! This silly holiday dares people to break one of the oldest superstitions around. Many folks believe opening umbrellas inside brings terrible luck, but today everyone ignores that rule on purpose. People snap photos of themselves with colorful umbrellas in kitchens, bedrooms, and classrooms. Some families make it a game to see who can open the most umbrellas indoors without feeling nervous about it!
activity_question: What superstition does this holiday encourage people to break?
activity_choices: Opening umbrellas indoors brings bad luck
Breaking mirrors causes seven years of trouble
Black cats crossing your path means doom
activity_answer: Opening umbrellas indoors brings bad luck
follow_up_question: What other silly superstitions do you think deserve their own "break the rule" holidays?

title: Paws for Heroes: National K9 Veterans Day
story: Dogs don't just fetch sticks and play catch – some serve as brave soldiers alongside human troops! National K9 Veterans Day on March 13 honors military and police dogs who risk their lives to protect people. These amazing canines detect bombs, track down bad guys, and even parachute into dangerous missions. On this special day, people share photos of hero dogs online, visit memorials dedicated to four-legged veterans, and donate to organizations that care for retired service dogs. Many police stations and military bases hold ceremonies where these incredible pups get the recognition they deserve with treats, medals, and lots of belly rubs!
activity_question: What are some jobs that K9 veterans do to help protect people?
activity_choices: Detect bombs and track down criminals
Deliver pizza and walk on tightropes
activity_answer: Detect bombs and track down criminals
bonus_fact: Some military dogs even learn to parachute from airplanes during missions!

title: It's Not My Fault Day!
story: Every March 13, people celebrate National Blame Someone Else Day by playfully pointing fingers at everyone but themselves! This silly holiday lets you blame your messy room on your pet, your late homework on aliens, or your missing socks on invisible sock monsters. People share funny blame posts on social media, create ridiculous excuses with friends, and enjoy a day of creative finger-pointing. Some families make it a game, coming up with the most outrageous reasons why things aren't their fault. It's all about having fun with silly excuses and laughing at how creative people can get when they don't want to take responsibility!
activity_question: What do people blame on invisible sock monsters during this holiday?
follow_up_question: What's the silliest excuse you've ever heard someone use?

title: Quack Attack: Donald Duck's Special Day
story: Donald Duck gets his own special day every March 13! Fans around the world celebrate this feisty cartoon character by watching his classic Disney movies and cartoons. Kids dress up in sailor suits and practice their best Donald Duck voice impressions. Disney parks often have special Donald Duck meet-and-greets, and stores sell his merchandise. Social media fills up with Donald Duck memes and videos of people trying to copy his famous angry quacking. Some families have Donald Duck movie marathons, complete with popcorn and Disney-themed snacks. It's the perfect day to embrace your inner grumpy duck and let out a good quack!
activity_question: What do kids often practice on Donald Duck Day?
bonus_fact: Donald Duck's middle name is actually Fauntleroy!
